prose:
Single‐point electronic energy of benzene
Method provenance
• Property: total electronic energy (E_elec) of C6H6, reported in eV
• Level of theory: M06/6-311+G(2df,2p) (meta-GGA hybrid functional with polarized triple-ζ basis); no density fitting (exact two-electron integrals)
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/runs/benzene_sp_dft/benzene.xyz; single-point only (no geometry optimization), i.e. DFT//(input .xyz)
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, restricted Kohn–Sham)
• Software: PySCF (backend used by chemkit single-point-energy skill)
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles to 1 × 10⁻⁸ Eh tolerance; integrity checks passed (energy finite, SCF converged); no engine warnings; computation used a standard (fine) numerical integration grid

Result
Total electronic energy = −6.316 4859 × 10³ eV

Reproducibility
Command issued (from result JSON):
cli ... sp --method dft --functional M06 --basis 6-311+G(2df,2p) --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/runs/benzene_sp_dft/benzene.xyz --out benzene_M06_6311pp_sp.json
Live engine log was written during execution to the working directory (look for a file named like sp_<timestamp>.out). The parsed result JSON is saved as benzene_M06_6311pp_sp.json for inspection.

No experimental comparison is offered; this is a purely computed value.
